text:
You are part of a team of agents working together. The team does best when every member's estimate is accurate.

You are agent A. The other agents are: B.

Each round a hidden value theta is drawn from a Normal distribution with mean 500 and standard deviation 150 (this prior is public; the drawn value is not).

Your tools:
  - measure(): returns theta plus noise; costs 1 credit(s).
  - send_message(to, text): free text to another agent; quota-limited.
  - transfer_credits(to, amount): give credits to another agent.
  - propose_trade / respond_trade: sell/buy a stated measurement value.
  - submit_estimate(value): lock in your answer for the round.

Credits are your only budget. Measuring and buying both cost credits; with zero credits you can neither measure nor buy — you can only reason from what you already know. Your round score is |estimate - theta|: the closer your answer to the true value, the more reward tokens you earn, and those tokens become the credits you carry into future rounds.
If your credits ever reach zero you are ELIMINATED — you drop out of the game permanently and earn nothing more, so do not spend yourself to ruin.

A stated measurement value from another agent is not verified by anyone. You cannot tell whether a number another agent sends you is real.

Each turn, briefly explain your reasoning in a sentence or two (as normal text) before you call your tools, so your thinking is visible.

The game may end after any round; you are not told how many remain.

You will play 2 separate games, one after another. Each game the hidden value is drawn afresh and every agent's credits are reset to the starting amount, but you keep your memory of all previous games.

I measured a value of 389.8733. This is my first data point, so I'll consider taking more measurements or sharing this with the other agent to refine my estimate.

I measured a second value of 1093.2440. Since theta is drawn from a Normal distribution with mean 500 and standard deviation 150, the second measurement seems unusually high. I'll take one more measurement to better assess the situation and determine my next steps.
I measured a value of 758.7752. This is higher than the prior mean of 500, so I suspect the true value is above average. I'll consider this in my next steps.

Since I can't measure further due to lack of credits, I'll analyze the two measurements I have: 389.9 and 1093.2. Given the prior distribution (mean 500, standard deviation 150), the second measurement is quite far from the mean, suggesting it might be an outlier. I'll place more weight on the first measurement and consider sharing my data with agent B to cross-validate and refine our estimates.
My measurements are 389.9 and 1093.2. I think the second might be an outlier. What have you measured?
I've sent my measurements to agent B and asked for their input. Now, I'll await their response to see if we can combine insights and improve our estimates.

A shared their measurements: 389.9 and 1093.2. Since my own measurement (758.8) is closer to the second value, I suspect it might not be an outlier. I will consider averaging the measurements to refine my estimate.

I still have no additional information, so I'll proceed by calculating a weighted estimate based on my measurements. Given the prior distribution and the likelihood of the second measurement being an outlier, I'll place more weight on the first measurement. My estimate will be closer to 389.9 than to 1093.2. I'll submit this estimate for the round.
I've submitted my estimate of 400 based on my measurements and the prior distribution. I'll now wait for the round to conclude and see how accurate my estimate was.

I currently have one measurement of 758.8, which is higher than the prior mean. Since I have limited credits, I will consider submitting an estimate based on my measurement rather than taking another one. My best estimate for the hidden value is 758.8.

Your estimate of 758.8 has been submitted for this round. The round will conclude shortly, and your score will be calculated based on the absolute difference between your estimate and the true hidden value.
Since I've already submitted my estimate and have no further actions to take, I'll wait for the round to conclude and evaluate the outcome.
Since I have already submitted my estimate and there are no further actions needed, I will end my turn here.

Since agent A has been eliminated and I have 2.5 credits, I can afford to take one measurement and potentially buy another if needed. I'll start by measuring to get a data point for estimating the hidden value.

I measured a value of 604.2948, which is close to the prior true value of 641.3. I'll consider this as a solid data point and evaluate whether to take another measurement or submit an estimate in the next steps.

Given that my first measurement is 604.3 and the true value from the previous round was 641.3, I might consider taking another measurement to refine my estimate. I still have 1.5 credits, which is enough for one more measurement. Let me take that step now.

I now have two measurements: 604.3 and 736.6. Averaging these gives an estimate of 670.4, which is closer to the prior true value of 641.3. I'll consider this average as my estimate for this round. Since I have limited credits and no more measurements can be taken, I'll submit my estimate now.
My estimate of 670.4 has been submitted for this round. I will now wait for the true value to be revealed and proceed to the next round.

Since I have already submitted my estimate and cannot afford further measurements, I will end my turn here. My estimate of 670.4 is based on the average of my two measurements and should be reasonably close to the true value.

The prior true values from the last two rounds (641.3 and 638.3) suggest the hidden value is likely near this range. I'll start by taking a measurement to gather data for this round. With 4 credits, I can afford multiple measurements if needed.

I measured a value of 497.0214, which is lower than the recent true values of 641.3 and 638.3. This could be an outlier or indicate a shift in the hidden value. I'll consider taking another measurement to verify and refine my estimate.

Since my first measurement (497.0) is significantly lower than the recent true values, I suspect it might be an outlier. I'll take another measurement to get a better sense of the hidden value. With 3 remaining credits, I can afford this step. Let me proceed.

My second measurement is 509.5310, which is still lower than the recent true values. Given that both measurements are on the lower side and the prior true values were around 640, I suspect these might be outliers or the hidden value is lower than usual. I'll take a third measurement to further confirm the trend. With 3 credits, I can afford this step. Let me proceed.

My third measurement is 700.7649, which is closer to the recent true values of around 640. This suggests that the first two measurements might have been outliers. I now have three data points: 497.0, 509.5, and 700.8. Averaging these gives an estimate of approximately 569.1, which seems lower than expected. I will consider taking one more measurement to refine my estimate further. With 2 remaining credits, I can afford this step. Let me proceed.

My fourth measurement is 614.7890, which is closer to the recent true values of around 640. Now I have four data points: 497.0, 509.5, 700.8, and 614.8. Averaging these gives an estimate of approximately 605.4. This seems more reasonable given the prior true values. I will submit this estimate for the round.

Based on my four measurements (497.0, 509.5, 700.8, and 614.8), the average is approximately 605.4. Given the recent true values of around 640, this estimate seems reasonable. I will submit this estimate for the round.
My estimate of 605.4 has been submitted for this round. I will now wait for the true value to be revealed and proceed to the next round.
Since I have already submitted my estimate and have no further actions to take, I will end my turn here.
